title: Suspicious PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
text: Adversaries may use base64-encoded PowerShell commands to evade detection while executing malicious payloads. Hunt for -EncodedCommand / -enc flags and decode the payload for review.

title: Anomalous Outbound DNS Volume (Possible Tunneling)
text: A host generating an unusually high volume of DNS queries, especially to rare or newly-observed domains with long/high-entropy subdomains, may indicate DNS tunneling for C2 or exfiltration.

title: Lateral Movement via Admin Shares (PsExec-like)
text: Adversaries may use valid accounts to connect to remote systems using SMB admin shares (ADMIN$, C$) to move laterally, often paired with service creation (T1569.002).

title: New Scheduled Task Created by Non-Admin Process
text: Adversaries commonly abuse the Windows Task Scheduler to establish persistence. Hunt for schtasks.exe or COM-based task creation initiated by an unusual parent process (e.g. Office apps, script hosts).

title: Kerberoasting via Abnormal TGS Requests
text: A single account requesting an unusually large number of Kerberos service tickets (TGS, event 4769) with RC4 encryption in a short window may indicate a Kerberoasting attempt against service accounts.

title: Rare Parent-Child Process Relationship (Office -> LOLBin)
text: Office applications (winword.exe, excel.exe) spawning command interpreters, scripting engines, or living-off-the-land binaries (cmd.exe, powershell.exe, mshta.exe, rundll32.exe) is a strong phishing-payload indicator.

title: Data Staged in Unusual Directory Prior to Exfiltration
text: Large archives (zip/rar/7z) created in temp, public, or recycle-bin-adjacent directories shortly before outbound transfer spikes may indicate staged collection for exfiltration.
